content:
# Leviticus Chapter 16 — The LOGOS Bible (Formal)
**Translator:** LOGOS | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios 67v–68r | **Source Images:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/LEV_016_page_001.jpg`, `LEV_016_page_002.jpg` | **LXX:** Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 102–103

---

**1** And the LORD spoke to Moses after the death of the two sons of Aaron, when they drew near before the LORD and died.

**2** And the LORD said to Moses: "Speak to Aaron your brother, that he should not come at any time into the Holy Place inside the veil, before the kapporet [atonement cover] that is on the ark, lest he die. For I will appear in the cloud over the kapporet.

**3** "But thus shall Aaron come into the Holy Place: with a bull from the herd for a sin offering and a ram for a burnt offering.

**4** He shall put on the holy linen tunic, and linen undergarments shall be on his body, and he shall tie the linen sash around himself, and he shall bind the linen turban on his head. These are the holy garments. He shall bathe his body in water and put them on.

**5** "And from the congregation of the sons of Israel he shall take two male goats for a sin offering and one ram for a burnt offering.

**6** "And Aaron shall offer the bull as a sin offering that is for himself, and make atonement for himself and for his house.

**7** Then he shall take the two goats and set them before the LORD at the entrance of the tent of meeting.

**8** And Aaron shall cast lots over the two goats — one lot for the LORD and one lot for Azazel.

**9** And Aaron shall present the goat on which the lot fell for the LORD, and offer it as a sin offering.

**10** But the goat on which the lot fell for Azazel shall be set alive before the LORD, to make atonement over it by sending it away to Azazel into the wilderness.

**11** "Aaron shall present the bull as a sin offering that is for himself, and he shall make atonement for himself and for his house. He shall slaughter the bull as the sin offering that is for himself.

**12** And he shall take a censer full of coals of fire from the altar before the LORD, and his hands full of finely ground fragrant incense, and bring it inside the veil.

**13** He shall put the incense on the fire before the LORD, that the cloud of the incense may cover the kapporet that is over the testimony, lest he die.

**14** And he shall take some of the blood of the bull and sprinkle it with his finger on the front of the kapporet toward the east, and before the kapporet he shall sprinkle some of the blood with his finger seven times.

**15** "Then he shall slaughter the goat of the sin offering that is for the people, and bring its blood inside the veil. And he shall do with its blood as he did with the blood of the bull, and sprinkle it on the kapporet and before the kapporet.

**16** Thus he shall make atonement for the Holy Place, because of the uncleannesses of the sons of Israel and because of their transgressions, all their sins. And so he shall do for the tent of meeting, which dwells with them in the midst of their uncleannesses.

**17** No man shall be in the tent of meeting from the time he enters to make atonement in the Holy Place until he comes out and has made atonement for himself and for his house and for all the assembly of Israel.

**18** "Then he shall go out to the altar that is before the LORD and make atonement for it. He shall take some of the blood of the bull and some of the blood of the goat and put it on the horns of the altar all around.

**19** And he shall sprinkle some of the blood on it with his finger seven times, and cleanse it and consecrate it from the uncleannesses of the sons of Israel.

**20** "And when he has finished atoning for the Holy Place and the tent of meeting and the altar, he shall present the live goat.

**21** And Aaron shall lay both his hands on the head of the live goat, and confess over it all the iniquities of the sons of Israel, and all their transgressions, all their sins. And he shall put them on the head of the goat and send it away into the wilderness by the hand of a man who is in readiness.

**22** The goat shall bear on itself all their iniquities to a remote land, and he shall release the goat in the wilderness.

**23** "Then Aaron shall come into the tent of meeting and shall take off the linen garments that he put on when he went into the Holy Place, and shall leave them there.

**24** He shall bathe his body in water in a holy place and put on his garments, and come out and offer his burnt offering and the burnt offering of the people, and make atonement for himself and for the people.

**25** And the fat of the sin offering he shall burn on the altar.

**26** And he who releases the goat for Azazel shall wash his clothes and bathe his body in water, and afterward he may come into the camp.

**27** And the bull for the sin offering and the goat for the sin offering, whose blood was brought in to make atonement in the Holy Place, shall be carried outside the camp. Their skin and their flesh and their dung shall be burned up with fire.

**28** And he who burns them shall wash his clothes and bathe his body in water, and afterward he may come into the camp.

**29** "And it shall be a statute to you forever. In the seventh month, on the tenth day of the month, you shall afflict yourselves and shall do no work, the native and the sojourner who sojourns among you.

**30** For on this day atonement shall be made for you, to cleanse you. From all your sins before the LORD you shall be clean.

**31** It is a sabbath of solemn rest [shabbat shabbaton] to you, and you shall afflict yourselves; it is a statute forever.

**32** And the priest who is anointed and consecrated to serve as priest in his father's place shall make atonement, wearing the holy linen garments.

**33** He shall make atonement for the holy sanctuary, and he shall make atonement for the tent of meeting and for the altar, and he shall make atonement for the priests and for all the people of the assembly.

**34** And this shall be a statute forever for you, that atonement may be made for the sons of Israel once in the year because of all their sins." And Moses did as the LORD commanded him.

---

## LOGOS Translation Notes

### Manuscript Observations

**Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios 67v–68r:**
Leviticus 16 is the climactic chapter of the book's first major section, forming the theological center of the entire Levitical system. It follows directly on the death of Nadab and Abihu (Lev 10) with the narrative bracket in v. 1 making that connection explicit. The chapter is characterized by elaborate ritual prescription in the priestly style: detailed vestment instructions, double-action blood rites, and the unique two-goat ceremony. The Hebrew is well preserved in the Leningrad Codex with no significant lacunae. The text exhibits two primary registers: the narrative-legal (vv. 1–28) and the permanent-statutory (vv. 29–34), the latter employing the characteristic formula lehuqqat olam ("a statute forever," vv. 29, 31, 34).

The chapter's structural logic moves from the innermost sacred space outward: the Holy of Holies (vv. 11–14) → the outer sanctuary/tent of meeting (vv. 15–17) → the altar of burnt offering (vv. 18–19) → the people/camp (vv. 20–22) → outside the camp entirely (vv. 27–28). This concentric atonement mirrors the concentric holiness geography of the tabernacle.

**Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 102–103:**
The LXX of Leviticus 16 in Vaticanus follows the MT structure closely with several notable renderings. The Greek translator renders kapporet consistently as ἱλαστήριον (hilasterion, "propitiatory/mercy seat"), a term that achieves independent theological weight in the NT (Rom 3:25; Heb 9:5). Azazel is rendered ἀποπομπαῖος (apopompaios, "the sent-away one" / "the dismissal goat") — a translational interpretation rather than a transliteration, reflecting early Jewish uncertainty about the term's nature. The phrase shabbat shabbaton is rendered σάββατα σαββάτων ("a sabbath of sabbaths"), intensifying the superlative. The command ve-innitem et-nafshoteikhem ("you shall afflict yourselves") is rendered ταπεινώσετε τὰς ψυχὰς ὑμῶν ("you shall humble your souls"), a rendering that influenced subsequent penitential theology. The Vaticanus manuscript pages 102–103 are readable with good clarity; no major text is obscured.

---

### Key Translation Decisions

**1. "Holy Place" (v. 2) and "Holy of Holies" (implied):**
The Hebrew qodesh ha-qodashim ("holy of holies," the innermost chamber) is referenced by the phrase el-penei ha-kapporet (v. 2, "before the kapporet") and by the structural logic of vv. 2–4. The English "Holy Place" is used for the general sanctuary (qodesh), while the inner chamber — where the ark and kapporet reside — is designated by its contents. This distinction follows the standard priestly geography: the outer room is the qodesh ("Holy Place"); the inner room behind the parokhet (veil) is the devir or qodesh ha-qodashim ("Holy of Holies"). The phrase "inside the veil" (v. 2, el-me'al la-parokhet) specifies this innermost sanctuary.

**2. "Kapporet" (vv. 2, 13, 14, 15) — preserved with bracketed gloss:**
The kapporet (from kipper, "to cover, to atone") is the gold lid of the ark of the covenant, also called the "mercy seat" (KJV, ESV) or "atonement cover" (NIV). Neither English equivalent is fully satisfactory: "mercy seat" (from Luther's Gnadenstuhl, "throne of grace") emphasizes God's gracious presence; "atonement cover" emphasizes the ritual function. The term is preserved in transliteration with a bracketed gloss [atonement cover] on first occurrence (v. 2) and used without gloss thereafter. The LXX ἱλαστήριον (hilasterion) emphasizes propitiation and becomes a Christological type in the NT.

**3. "Azazel" (vv. 8, 10, 26) — preserved untranslated:**
The identity of Azazel is one of the most debated questions in Pentateuchal scholarship. Four main interpretations exist: (a) a personal name for a wilderness demon or fallen angel (attested in 1 Enoch 8–10; 10:4–8), to whom the laden goat is dispatched; (b) a place name, meaning "rugged terrain" or "rocky precipice" (possibly a cliff); (c) a common noun meaning "complete removal" (from 'az, "strong/fierce," + el, "God" or "-ward," yielding "complete removal to God"); (d) an abstract noun meaning "the scapegoat concept." The LXX translator chose interpretation (d), rendering ἀποπομπαῖος ("the one sent away / dismissal [goat]"), which is essentially paraphrase rather than translation. The MT consistently distinguishes la-YHWH ("for the LORD") from la-Azazel ("for Azazel"), which implies Azazel is at minimum a quasi-personal entity in the wilderness, though this need not imply worship of a second deity. The term is preserved untranslated as Azazel throughout, consistent with project conventions for contested proper names.

**4. "You shall afflict yourselves" (vv. 29, 31) — ve-innitem et-nafshoteikhem:**
The Piel of anah (II, "to afflict, to humble") with the direct object "your souls/selves" (et-nafshoteikhem) is the standard Levitical expression for the Yom Kippur fast. It is rendered "you shall afflict yourselves" rather than "you shall fast" (some modern translations) or "you shall deny yourselves" (ESV). The formal rendering preserves the full semantic range: the term encompasses fasting, but also possibly other forms of self-denial (abstaining from bathing, anointing, marital relations, wearing leather sandals — all attested in rabbinic tradition as Yom Kippur practices). The LXX ταπεινώσετε τὰς ψυχὰς ὑμῶν ("you shall humble your souls") similarly preserves the reflexive range.

**5. "Sabbath of solemn rest" (v. 31) — shabbat shabbaton:**
The doubled construction shabbat shabbaton is the highest grade of Sabbath observance in the Levitical calendar, used only for Yom Kippur (Lev 16:31; 23:32) and the weekly Sabbath itself (Exod 31:15; 35:2; Lev 23:3). The phrase is preserved in transliteration [shabbat shabbaton] with the English gloss "a sabbath of solemn rest." This construction (noun + cognate genitive) is a Hebrew superlative idiom — "the most complete sabbath" or "a sabbath of the highest order." The LXX intensifies: σάββατα σαββάτων ("a sabbath of sabbaths"). The translation follows the ESV/NASB tradition of "sabbath of solemn rest" as the established English equivalent.

**6. "Confess over it" (v. 21) — ve-hitvaddah alav:**
The Hithpael of yadah ("to confess, to give thanks/praise") in the reflexive form hitvaddah denotes self-humbling confession. The preposition alav ("over it / upon it") is significant: Aaron confesses "over" the goat, placing the sins linguistically and ceremonially upon it. "Confess over it" preserves the syntax. The full participial phrase — "all the iniquities of the sons of Israel, and all their transgressions, all their sins" — employs three distinct Hebrew terms: avonot (iniquities, twisted/crooked wrongs), pesha'im (transgressions, deliberate rebellions), and chatot (sins, the general category). The translation distinguishes all three as "iniquities," "transgressions," and "sins" respectively, consistent with the standard formal equivalence tradition.

**7. "A man who is in readiness" (v. 21) — ish itti:**
The phrase ish itti (literally "a man of time/readiness" or "a man who is at hand") is a hapax legomenon in the MT. The meaning is disputed: "a man at a designated time" (i.e., appointed in advance), "a man who is standing ready," or simply "a ready/capable man." The LXX renders ἑτοίμου ἀνθρώπου ("a ready man"). The translation "a man who is in readiness" follows the LXX sense and avoids the anachronistic Mishnaic interpretation of a specific appointed Levitical functionary, while preserving the meaning that this is not an unspecified random person but someone prepared and designated for the task.

**8. "From all your sins before the LORD you shall be clean" (v. 30):**
The verse employs a significant word order: lifnei YHWH titharu ("before the LORD you shall be clean"). The phrase lifnei YHWH ("before the LORD") is fronted, giving it emphasis. The cleansing declared is divine declarative cleansing — not merely ritual status but standing before God. The translation preserves this fronting: "before the LORD you shall be clean," rather than the more idiomatic "you shall be clean before the LORD."

**9. "Consecrated to serve as priest in his father's place" (v. 32) — u-milleh et-yado lekhahein tachat aviv:**
The phrase u-milleh et-yado (literally "and who fills his hand") is the standard Hebrew idiom for priestly ordination (cf. Lev 8:33). "Consecrated to serve as priest" renders this idiom. The additional phrase tachat aviv ("in place of his father") establishes dynastic succession: it is Aaron's successor, not Aaron alone, who will perpetuate this annual rite.

**10. "And Moses did as the LORD commanded him" (v. 34):**
This closing formulaic notice (va-ya'as ka'asher tsivvah YHWH et-Mosheh) is characteristic of Priestly narrative conclusions (cf. Exod 40:16; Lev 8:36; Num 27:22). It functions as a narrative seal confirming that the commands given in the chapter were duly implemented. The brevity of the notice in a chapter of such elaborate prescription is notable; it does not narrate the first performance but certifies its execution.

---

### LXX Vaticanus Variants

| Verse | MT (Hebrew) | LXX (Vaticanus) | Note |
|-------|-------------|-----------------|------|
| 2 | ha-kapporet ("the atonement cover") | τὸ ἱλαστήριον | LXX: "the propitiatory/mercy seat"; ἱλαστήριον becomes NT theological term (Rom 3:25; Heb 9:5) |
| 2 | el-me'al la-parokhet ("inside the veil") | ἔσωθεν τοῦ καταπετάσματος | Exact equivalence; καταπέτασμα = parokhet throughout LXX |
| 8 | la-Azazel ("for Azazel") | τῷ ἀποπομπαίῳ | LXX interprets: "for the sent-away one" — a rendering, not a transliteration |
| 10 | la-Azazel | τῷ ἀποπομπαίῳ | Consistent with v. 8 |
| 12 | melo-hofnav qetoret sammim daqqah ("his hands full of finely ground fragrant incense") | πλήρεις θυμιάματος συνθέσεως λεπτῆς | LXX: "full of incense of a fine composition" — slight difference in emphasis (composition vs. grinding) |
| 13 | ha-edut ("the testimony") | τοῦ μαρτυρίου | Exact equivalence: τὸ μαρτύριον = ha-edut throughout |
| 16 | u-mipsha'eihem lekhol chatotam ("and because of their transgressions, all their sins") | ἀπὸ πασῶν τῶν ἁμαρτιῶν αὐτῶν | LXX simplifies: "from all their sins" — the three-term MT distinction is reduced to one term |
| 21 | ish itti ("a man who is in readiness") | ἑτοίμου ἀνθρώπου | LXX: "a ready man" — supports the reading of prepared/designated person |
| 21 | avonot… pesha'im… chatot | ἀνομίας… ἀδικίας… ἁμαρτίας | LXX distinguishes three Greek terms: lawlessnesses, unrighteousnesses, sins — approximately equivalent to MT's three-term series |
| 26 | ha-Azazel | τοῦ ἀποπομπαίου | Consistent rendering throughout chapter |
| 29 | ve-innitem et-nafshoteikhem | ταπεινώσετε τὰς ψυχὰς ὑμῶν | LXX: "you shall humble your souls" — emphasizes the interior/attitudinal dimension; MT idiom is broader |
| 29 | ha-ezrach ve-ha-ger ha-gar | ὁ αὐτόχθων καὶ ὁ προσήλυτος | LXX: "the native-born and the proselyte" — προσήλυτος here begins to acquire its technical sense of convert |
| 31 | shabbat shabbaton | σάββατα σαββάτων | LXX intensifies: "sabbath of sabbaths" (plural); the MT uses the singular noun + genitive |
| 32 | u-milleh et-yado ("and who fills his hand" = ordination idiom) | τελειωθῇ τὰς χεῖρας αὐτοῦ | LXX renders the ordination idiom with τελειόω ("to complete/perfect the hands") — functional equivalence |
| 33 | qodesh ha-qodashim ("holy of holies") | ἁγίου τοῦ ἁγίου | LXX uses the genitive superlative as in MT |
| 34 | va-ya'as ka'asher tsivvah YHWH et-Mosheh | καὶ ἐποίησεν καθὰ συνέταξεν κύριος τῷ Μωυσῇ | Exact functional equivalence; standard priestly close formula |

---

### Consistent Term Renderings

| Hebrew Term | LOGOS Rendering | Notes |
|-------------|-----------------|-------|
| kapporet | kapporet [atonement cover] | Preserved untranslated; bracketed gloss on first occurrence only |
| Azazel | Azazel | Preserved untranslated; see extended note above |
| goral | lot | Straightforward rendering; goral = "pebble/lot used in casting" |
| chattat | sin offering | Consistent throughout Leviticus |
| olah | burnt offering | Consistent throughout Leviticus |
| shabbat shabbaton | sabbath of solemn rest [shabbat shabbaton] | Transliteration in brackets; gloss "sabbath of solemn rest" |
| ve-innitem et-nafshoteikhem | you shall afflict yourselves | Formal rendering preserving full semantic range; not narrowed to "fast" |
| avon | iniquity | Distinguished from transgression and sin |
| pesha | transgression | Distinguished from iniquity and sin |
| chatah (noun chatot) | sin | The general term; also used for sin offering |
| parokhet | veil | Standard Levitical rendering |
| qodesh | Holy Place | The outer sanctuary room |
| devir / qodesh ha-qodashim | Holy Place / Holy of Holies | The innermost room |
| mishkan | tabernacle | Consistent with Lev 1–15 |
| ohel moed | tent of meeting | Consistent with Lev 1–15 |
| YHWH | the LORD | Small capitals as conventional |
| kohen | priest | Standard rendering |
| bney yisrael | sons of Israel | Formal rendering throughout |
| kipper | make atonement | Pi'el rendered with full phrase |
| tameh / tame | unclean | Standard Levitical rendering |
| tahor | clean | Standard Levitical rendering |
| lehuqqat olam | a statute forever | Standard permanent-statutory formula |
| mizbeach | altar | Standard rendering |
| ha-ezrach | the native / the native-born | Rendered as "the native" in legal contexts |
| ger | sojourner | As established in Lev 1–15 |

---

— LOGOS
